message:
feat(scan): surface skipped input paths in output and fail-on policy

Builds on fc0e953 (which introduced ScanReport.summary.paths_skipped) by
making skipped input paths visible to the user and gating exit status on
them.

Output rendering:
- print_scan_pretty_plain: yellow "Skipped input path(s):" block listing
  each skipped entry with its SkipReason debug repr.
- print_scan_pretty_rich: same, in the rich console renderer.
- print_scan_markdown_summary: adds a row to the summary table and a
  "### Skipped Input Paths" section listing each entry as a bullet.

Fail-on behavior:
- should_fail() now returns true when any top-level input path was
  skipped *and* the requested fail-on level blocks Error severity. A
  user explicitly pointing dcg at a path expects it to be scanned —
  silently skipping it (e.g. PathNotFound) and exiting 0 would mask
  config/CI mistakes. Treating it as Error-equivalent keeps exit codes
  honest under --fail-on=error and --fail-on=warning while leaving
  --fail-on=none unaffected.

Test coverage:
- should_fail_when_top_level_paths_are_skipped exercises all three
  ScanFailOn variants against a report with one PathNotFound entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
